title: Structure and tectonics of the central Chilean margin (31°–33°S): implications for subduction erosion and shallow crustal seismicity
abstract: Using wide-angle seismic refraction and multichannel seismic (MCS) reflection data at 31°–33°S, the team identified large-scale crustal thinning and long-term subsidence of the submarine forearc caused by subduction erosion processes. A trenchward dipping normal scarp marks the upper-to-middle continental slope transition. A prominent lateral velocity contrast propagates deep into the continental crust defining a major lateral seismic discontinuity associated with the collision of the Juan Fernández Ridge. The central Chilean margin shows a heterogeneous structure strongly controlled by the subduction of the Juan Fernández Ridge at ~33°S.

location.description: Offshore central Chile between 31°S and 33°S, Juan Fernández Ridge collision zone

data.name: Wide-angle OBS seismic refraction profiles (31°–33°S)
data.description: Wide-angle seismic refraction data recorded on OBS instruments along profiles at 31°–33°S offshore central Chile. Used for 2D P-wave tomography.

data.name: Multichannel seismic reflection profiles (31°–33°S)
data.description: MCS reflection data acquired offshore central Chile between 31°S and 33°S, imaging forearc structure and subduction erosion.

processing.summary: Combined wide-angle OBS refraction and multichannel seismic reflection data from offshore central Chile (31°–33°S) processed to produce velocity and structural models. Wide-angle data processed through 2D travel time tomography. MCS data processed through standard stack and migration workflow. Results integrated to characterize the crustal structure and document large-scale subduction erosion.
workflow: 1. Wide-angle OBS data: phase picking (Pg, PmP, Pn)
processing.notes: Bronze open access PDF available via Oxford Academic. Study covers Juan Fernández Ridge collision zone at ~33°S.
